title: Verify existing implementation of User model with password hash field
description: Verified that the User model with password hash field already exists. The Users Payload collection (src/collections/Users.ts) has auth:true which provides email, hash, and salt fields automatically via Payload's built-in auth. The User type in payload-types.ts includes hash?: string | null and salt?: string | null. The auth-service.ts (lines 45-60) provides PBKDF2 password verification (25000 iterations, sha256, 512 bits) matching Payload's algorithm. Plain-text passwords are never stored. The collection includes createdAt and updatedAt timestamps. Unit tests for password hashing and verification exist in src/auth/user-store.test.ts (lines 33-106) covering: hash differs from plaintext, same input produces different hashes (salted), correct password returns true, incorrect returns false. The only deviation from task description is the field is named 'hash' not 'passwordHash' - Payload's built-in auth uses 'hash' as the conventional field name.
existing_patterns: src/collections/Users.ts - Payload collection with auth:true providing hash/salt fields
src/auth/auth-service.ts - PBKDF2 password verification (25000 iterations, sha256)
src/auth/user-store.ts - User interface with passwordHash and salt fields, hashPassword and verifyPassword methods
src/auth/user-store.test.ts - Unit tests for password hashing and verification
